body.title: CC task listener: background loop reads GRANNY_DISPATCH → auto-sprint
body.result: cc_task_listener.py: polls shared channel, dispatches via cc_queue, posts GRANNY_ACK; HWM dedup; 9 tests
body.description:
For fully autonomous factory mode, a background CC session needs to watch the shared channel for GRANNY_DISPATCH messages from Granny and auto-sprint the assigned tickets. The listener: (1) polls channel_messages table (or JSONL fallback) for new GRANNY_DISPATCH|ticket=T-xxx messages, (2) calls cc_queue.py dispatch T-xxx (idempotent — already in_progress is OK), (3) invokes the sprint-ticket skill for each dispatched ticket (or queues them), (4) posts completion back to channel as GRANNY_COMPLETE|ticket=T-xxx.

This can run inside the existing superclaude/CC tmux session as a periodic background habit, or as a standalone watcher script at lab/claudecode/cc_task_listener.py.

The listener enables the factory: Granny routes → channel → CC picks up → sprints → closes → Granny routes next.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_task_listener.py (new), or a new habit in CC's scheduler
**Design rules:** reads channel via Postgres (channel_messages table); idempotent dispatch; never double-sprint
**Scope boundary:** reading GRANNY_DISPATCH messages + triggering sprint only. Does not do sprint work inline — delegates to /sprint-ticket flow.
**Test plan:** insert a GRANNY_DISPATCH row into channel_messages; run one listener poll cycle; assert cc_queue shows ticket in_progress
